ephemeral · anonymous · end-to-end encrypted
someone wants to talk
stranger from somewhere
Live mesh

You are one of 0.

Every dot is a real One Link node online right now. The colors are the coherence field that decides how packets find each other. No IPs. No usernames. No locations beyond approximate region. The moment your session opens, a new dot lights up.

nodes0
relays0
fieldτ_c routing
sweep60 Hz
youconnecting...
What you are seeing

The tau_c coherence field.

One Link does not use a routing table. It uses a continuous field. Each peer's local view of the field is a small ripple in a damped Helmholtz oscillator. Packets follow the field gradient toward their destination, the same way water finds the low point of a hill.

When a relay disappears, the field re-settles in a single round. When new peers join, their ripples integrate into the field. The math is the same equations that describe quantum coherence and biological membranes. We borrowed the idea from physics.

The colormap on the canvas above is the field's energy state, sampled at 60 Hz, smoothed for the eye. The dots are peers. The brighter the local field, the more traffic is flowing through that region.

// solver, one timestep
field = helmholtz_step(
  prev = field,
  peers = active_nodes,
  dt = 1 / 60,
  gamma = damping,
  k = coherence_freq
)

routes = gradient_descent(
  field,
  sources = peers,
  destinations = peers
)

// 10,000 peers in 1.74 ms (CPU; GPU path lands v0.22)
What the map keeps to you

The shape, not the signature.

Just presence.

The map shows that you and others are here. The relays carry sealed envelopes between peers; identifiers stay on the devices that hold them.

Opaque peer IDs.

The dots are anonymous, rotating peer IDs. No usernames, no profiles. Recognition, when it happens, happens through pairing, never through the map.

Continental shape.

Dots cluster by approximate continental region derived from relay topology. The shape of the network, not the precise location of any person.

Join the mesh

Light up a new dot.

Install One Link and your device becomes part of the field. Every new node strengthens routing for everyone else.

Get One Link → Run a relay
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
